label: Intercom
description: Connect to Intercom to read conversations, contacts, and help center articles from your workspace.

form.title: Connect Intercom
form.subtitle: Paste your Intercom Access Token to give Anton access to conversations, contacts, and your help center.

methods.label: Access Token
methods.description: Paste a permanent Access Token from the Intercom Developer Hub. No OAuth flow needed — copy, paste, done. Works for both personal and team workspaces.
methods.how_to:
## Generate an Intercom Access Token

### Steps

1. Open [Intercom Developer Hub](https://app.intercom.com/a/developer-signup) and sign in.
2. In the top-right, click your avatar → **Your apps** (or go directly to [developers.intercom.com/building-apps](https://developers.intercom.com/building-apps)).
3. Select an existing app or click **New app** — give it a name like `Anton`.
4. In the left sidebar, click **Authentication**.
5. Under **Access token**, click **Generate token** if one doesn't already exist.
6. Copy the token — it begins with `dG9r...` (base64) or a similar prefix.
7. Paste it into the **Access Token** field below.
8. Choose your workspace **Region** (US, EU, or AU) — this must match where your Intercom data is hosted.

### Notes

- The token is tied to the app and grants access to whichever workspace the app is installed in.
- Tokens do not expire but can be rotated from the same Authentication page.
- If you need narrower access, create a dedicated app with only the required scopes enabled under **Permissions** before generating the token.
- EU and AU regions have separate API base URLs — Anton uses your region selection to route requests correctly.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Found in Developer Hub → Your app → Authentication. Rotate it there if compromised.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Region
fields.description: Select the region where your Intercom workspace is hosted. Must match your account's data residency setting.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Install App)
methods.description: Use your own Intercom OAuth client to authorize via browser. Best for multi-workspace or production integrations where you want users to grant access through Intercom's standard install flow.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 — Bring Your Own OAuth Client

This method opens Intercom's standard app installation flow in your browser. You need an Intercom app with OAuth enabled in the Developer Hub.

### Steps

1. Go to [Intercom Developer Hub](https://app.intercom.com/a/developer-signup) and open or create your app.
2. In the left sidebar, click **Authentication**.
3. Enable **Use OAuth** and add `http://localhost` (or your redirect URI) under **Redirect URLs**.
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown on the Authentication page.
5. Paste them into the fields below.
6. Select your workspace **Region**.
7. Click **Submit** — a browser window will open Intercom's consent screen.
8. Authorize the app and Anton will store the resulting access token.

### Notes

- Intercom OAuth tokens do not expire by default for installed apps, but can be revoked by the workspace admin.
- Scopes are configured on the app's **Permissions** page in the Developer Hub — set them before running the OAuth flow.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Developer Hub → Your app → Authentication → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: From Developer Hub → Your app → Authentication → Client Secret.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Region
fields.description: Select the region where your Intercom workspace is hosted.
